segments.text: Call if you want, but who is going to answer you? Which angel are you going to turn to?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Surely anger slays the fool and jealousy kills the simple.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I have seen a fool growing strong,  but I immediately cursed his house.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: His sons are never safe;  they are crushed in court with no one to defend them.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The hungry eat everything he has harvested, taking even that protected by a thorn hedge,  while others look to steal his wealth.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For evil doesn't come from the dust; neither does trouble grow from the earth.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But human beings are born for trouble just as certainly as sparks from a fire fly upwards.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If it were me, I would go to God and put my case before him.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He is the one who does amazing, incredible things; miracles that can't be counted!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He gives rain to the earth and sends water to the fields.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He exalts the humble, and rescues those who mourn.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He frustrates the plans of the cunning so that they are unsuccessful.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He traps the wise in their own clever thinking, and the schemes of twisted people are cut short.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: In the daytime they're in the dark, and they stumble around at noon like it's night.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But God is the one who saves from their cutting remarks,  and the poor from the actions of the powerful.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: As a result those who are helpless have hope, and the wicked have to shut their mouths!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: See how happy is the person God corrects—so don't despise the Almighty's discipline.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For he causes pain but he provides relief; he wounds but his hands heal.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He will save you from many disasters; a multitude of evils will not affect you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: In times of famine he will rescue you from death, and in times of war he will save you from the power of the sword.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You will be protected from sharp-tongued slander; and when violence comes you will not be afraid.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You will laugh at violence and famine; you won't be afraid of wild animals—
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: for you will be at peace with the stones of the field and the wild animals will be at peace with you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You will be certain that your home is safe, for you will go to where you live and find nothing missing.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You will also be sure that you will have many children; your descendants will be like the grass of the earth.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You will live to a ripe old age like a sheaf of grain when it is harvested.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Look, we've examined it, and it's true! Listen to what I'm saying and apply it to yourself!
segments.speaker: Unknown
